This workshop, led by music instructor Meghan Gallant, will cover fundamentals of ukulele playing: the parts of a ukulele, tuning, strumming patterns, and some basic chords. By the end of this class, you'll have learned the skills you need to start playing your first songs on the ukulele! Beginner friendly, no previous experience is necessary. events.title: The SpongeBob Movie: Search for SquarePants
events.description: Desperate to be a big guy, SpongeBob sets out to prove his bravery to Mr. Krabs by following The Flying Dutchman - a mysterious swashbuckling ghost pirate - on a seafaring comedy adventure that takes him to the deepest depths of the deep sea, where no Sponge has gone before. Drop in! Rated G. Daycares and Groups please phone 416-393-7746 to register. Please indicate group size.

events.title: Che-Mystery
events.description: Hands-on chemical science! Kids explore physical and chemical changes through exciting experiments. They’ll watch crystals form, see acetone turn foam to goo, play with non-Newtonian fluids, and use hydrophobic compounds to stay dry, uncovering the fascinating science behind everyday reactions in a hands-on adventure. Take-home: Silly Putty For kids ages 6-12. Space is limited. Gentle Reminder:  Due to high demand, we kindly ask you to arrive on time so everyone has a chance to participate.

The Blue Willow pattern on the tea set catches Jane’s attention … will that be the inspiration she’s looking for?​” Catherine Little presents a reading from her picture book "Jane and the Blue Willow Princess" about author Jane Austen, then leads a fun craft with air-dry clay.

events.title: Saturday Movie: The Secret Garden
events.description: Presenting the film version of  The Secret Garden , published in 1911 by Frances Hodgson Burnett, a British/American beloved author of children's literature. This classic novel centers on the physical and emotional transformation of two sickly, neglected children through the healing power of nature and friendship.  Starring Colin Firth.  Everyone is welcome to this inclusive screening. Drop-in! No registration required.

events.title: Your Library Soundtrack: AI and Music Production Closing Showcase
events.description: Over the past 12 weeks, participants in TPL's Innovator in Residence (AI and Music Production) program have been exploring how artificial intelligence can enhance and amplify their own music creativity. They've learned that rather than making music for you, AI can offer you new ways to develop and realize original ideas, regardless of music experience. They've also learned how to use these tools responsibly, including how to navigate the ethical considerations that come with AI-generated music.

events.title: 3D Printing Demo
events.description: Watch a 3D printer in action, see samples, learn how they work, and how you can use them at TPL Digital Innovation Hubs. This is a drop-in demo, come see us anytime during the hour!

events.title: Making Your First Game
events.description: Interested in what makes a video game? Join us for this lecture by TMU Professor Fitzgerald, and learn about game engines, creating a minimum viable product and the importance of game testing. Children under 13 must be accompanied by an adult. Registration begins June 27th, 2026 at 12:30 pm, drop-ins are welcome. Python is an open source programming language that is used for creating web applications, web content, mathematics and system scripting. In this course you will learn how python code works and given time to practice. Prior coding knowledge is not necessary for this introductory class, but you should be confident with using a computer. Key takeaways will include a working knowledge of variables, loops, conditional ar

events.title: Henna Art for Teens

events.title: Introduction to Serger Sewing
events.description: Learn the basics of operating our serger, the Juki MO-735!   In this class we will go over the basic operation and key functions of a serger sewing machine. Threading the machine and safety will be discussed. Between three and five threads are routinely used to sew a seam, trim off the raw edges & finish the edges all at the same time with the Juki MO-735 serger.  However, this workshop focuses solely on learning the 4-thread overlock stitch. events.title: Family Movie: GOAT

Will's new teammates aren't thrilled about having a little goat on their roster, but Will is determined to revolutionize the sport and prove once and for all that 'smalls can ball! events.title: Vector Design with Adobe Illustrator
events.description: Learn the ins and outs of vector design using Adobe Illustrator CC. We will talk about the differences between vector and raster images, explore some of the beginner tools at our disposal, and then use these tools to design a small vector drawing. This class is for beginners but assumes you have a basic understanding of computer skills. This is a drop in program for patrons of all ages. Computers will be provided but space is limited.

events.title: Disassemble a Computer Workshop
events.description: Have you ever wondered what's going on inside a computer? Ever wanted to take apart a computer to see? This workshop is your chance! Free Geek Toronto is a non-profit organization dedicated to getting unwanted computers and electronics back into working condition. During this workshop, we'll go over what's inside a computer, how to properly open a desktop, and safety guidelines to follow when it comes to working with electronics. Registration is required. Registration begins on Monday, July 27.

Space is limited. Please arrive at 3:30pm to secure your spot in the program. Participants must be available both dates to ensure completion of project.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutrit

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Youth Hub: DIY Stained Glass Art
events.description: Capture the sun and welcome springtime weather with your own dazzling masterpiece, created with a glass panel and paint. All supplies provided. Space is limited, so arrive early to ensure your spot. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Port Union Chess Club
events.description: Looking for a fun after-school activity? Join our Youth Chess Program to learn new skills, play matches, and connect with others!  This program is open to youth ages 7-17 years old. Facilitated by the University of Toronto Scarborough Chess Club. No registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Soundscape
events.description: Next we will be inspired by the library’s architecture and happenings as we are guided by musician Aniqa Qadir to create a soundscape. Working in groups, visitors will be prompted to absorb the sounds of the building and led through the process of creating an audio journey. This playful session welcomes participants to break the whisper norm of a library and interrupt the space with their observations. Led by Aniqa Qadir and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Taxation and Government Benefits
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop will provide guidance on tax filing, understanding available benefits and credits and its eligibility requirements, and how to access applicable government financial supports. Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Cercle de Conversation Française / French Conversation Circle
events.description: Nous vous invitons à pratiquer votre français dans un environnement informel ! Tous les niveaux sont bienvenus. We invite you to practice your French in an informal environment! This French Conversation Circle is open to all levels. Accessibilité: Le sous-titrage en direct ou automatique, l’interprétation en langue des signes, les services d’intervenant, ou les services d’accompagnement sont disponibles sur demande. Veuillez soumettre votre demande au moins deux semaines à l’avance à : tpl.ca/ac

events.title: Knitting Circle
events.description: Join Port Union Library's Knitting Circle. Chat and knit or crochet with a friendly group! You can also share your projects with others!  This is a drop in program but limited to a room capacity of 20 participants. Arrive early to secure your spot.  No registration required.

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Creating Parts With Machinery This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno. We will also have some colouring and crafts for everyone Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around the Branch Tour
events.description: The Brookbanks branch and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry team to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Free program.  Registration will open up one month before the program date. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Presented as part of TPL’s environmental program series, Our Fragile P

events.title: Style Your Supplies: Pencil Case Painting for Teens
events.description: Get creative and design your own pencil case! Join us for a fun and creative session and take home a custom pencil case perfect for school or everyday use. All supplies are provided. Tuesday September 22 2026 Ages 13-17 years From 3-4 pm Drop in, first come first serve. Space is limited Humberwood Branch

events.description: Need somewhere new to prep for the new school year, study, or just hang out? Our program room has lots of space for you and your friends.

events.description: LivingWorks SafeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks SafeTALK certificat

events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Port Union Chess Club
events.description: Looking for a fun after-school activity? Join our Youth Chess Program to learn new skills, play matches, and connect with others!  This program is open to youth ages 7-17 years old. Facilitated by the University of Toronto Scarborough Chess Club. No registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Knitting Circle
events.description: Join Port Union Library's Knitting Circle. Chat and knit or crochet with a friendly group! You can also share your projects with others!  This is a drop in program but limited to a room capacity of 20 participants. Arrive early to secure your spot.  No registration required.

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno. We will also have some colouring and crafts for everyone Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
